hero.name: Cyprien Dcunha
hero.tagline: Senior backend engineer designing resilient, multi-cloud distributed systems — from uplifting Tier 1 core services onto GCP to LLM-driven financial platforms.
hero.currently: Uplifting core and platform services from AWS to GCP at Salesforce.

caseStudies.title: Core & Platform Services GCP Uplift
caseStudies.summary: Porting AWS-native core services onto GCP. First AWS-to-GCP uplifts in the platform org; four shipped so far.
caseStudies.problem: Core and platform services ran only on AWS. Each uplift meant standing up GCP infra, getting it production-ready, and moving live customers across.
approach: Set up GCP infra for each service in preprod and prod.
Ran production readiness sign-offs and operational tooling before taking traffic.
Coordinated upstream and downstream changes with dependency teams.
Migrated customers from AWS to GCP.
outcome: Shipped Key Broker, DocGen, PDFGen, etc. services on GCP.
First AWS-to-GCP uplifts in the platform team and wider org.

caseStudies.title: VendorHoldbacks Re-Architecture
caseStudies.summary: Decomposed a batch monolith into an event-driven microservice platform handling $2.1B in vendor payments at 2000 TPS.
caseStudies.problem: The VendorHoldbacks platform was a SQL-heavy batch monolith. Reconciliation ran overnight, scaling was pinned to a single database, and vendors were filing inquiries against payment decisions they couldn't see reasoning for.
approach: Split the monolith into 11 microservices. Led a team of 3 through design and rollout.
Replaced batch SQL with Lambda, SQS, SNS, and DynamoDB, managed via CloudFormation.
Co-led architecture reviews and cutover plans across upstream sources.
outcome: 6M transactions/day, $2.1B, 2000 TPS on the new platform.
Payment processing moved from overnight batches to near real-time.
Vendor inquiries dropped 23.5% across 14+ countries once decisions were visible same-day instead of next-day.

caseStudies.title: LLM-driven Financial Reconciliation Engine
caseStudies.summary: LLM reconciliation engine processing up to 10M records per day at 67% of human accuracy.
caseStudies.problem: Reconciliation was done manually by analysts matching records against historical customer notes. Didn't scale to 10M records/day.
approach: RAG pipeline over historical analyst notes, giving the LLM prior human decisions as context.
LLM-based semantic mapping for free-text fields that rule-based matchers couldn't handle.
Integrated into the reconciliation workflow with human review on low-confidence outputs.

caseStudies.title: VendorHoldbacks Rule Engine
caseStudies.summary: Self-service rule platform running 5000+ rules at 50ms and 2000 TPS.
caseStudies.problem: Holdback rules were hard-coded across services. Every new rule took ~4 weeks of engineering.
approach: Drools-backed rule engine.
Web portal for business owners to author, test, and publish rules.
Tuned indexing and caching to hold 50ms at 2000 TPS.
outcome: Rule authoring dropped from ~4 weeks of engineering to minutes.
All 5000+ production rules are now authored by the VHB business team.

caseStudies.title: Seasonal Holdbacks
caseStudies.summary: Linear-regression risk model for holdback decisions on 3,600+ high-risk vendors across peak-season events. Prevented $14.7MM in debit balance exposure.
caseStudies.problem: Peak events (Prime Day, Black Friday, holiday) needed per-vendor holdback adjustments. Analysts couldn't scale, so the default was conservative holdbacks that strained vendors.
approach: Pipeline over 90,000+ vendors' historical data to train a linear-regression risk model.
Picked linear regression for explainability to finance and vendor-facing teams. Stronger ML was on the roadmap.
outcome: Automated risk decisions for 3,600+ high-risk vendors through peak events.

caseStudies.title: Special Merchant Data Segregation
caseStudies.summary: Separated views, APIs, and reports between general vendors and special merchants across 20+ input sources. Config-driven onboarding.
caseStudies.problem: Stored data was already segregated between general vendors and special merchants, but downstream views, APIs, and legacy CSV/Excel reports weren't. Adding a new special merchant meant code changes across every layer.
approach: Split views by permission group.
Auth and validation at the API layer to scope responses to caller permissions.
Stripped restricted rows from legacy CSV and Excel reports at runtime.
Made new-merchant onboarding config-driven across the 20+ upstream sources.
outcome: Segregation held across views, APIs, and reports — not just storage.
New-merchant onboarding went from multi-team code changes to a config entry.

caseStudies.title: Approvals — Emails
caseStudies.summary: Policy-based email orchestrator for approval and reminder workflows. 200K+ emails/day.
caseStudies.problem: Approval workflows had no dedicated email layer. Policy-based routing, configurable cadence, and template control didn't exist anywhere in the stack.
approach: Greenfield policy-based orchestrator for approval and reminder emails.
Templates, routing, and cadence driven by policy.
outcome: Policy-based email control now exists as a first-class capability.

caseStudies.title: Stock Market Simulator
caseStudies.summary: C++ HFT market replay simulator at <50ns per-execution latency.
caseStudies.problem: The firm needed to backtest trading strategies against historical market data without running a full exchange simulator.
approach: C++17 replay engine with lock-free data structures on the hot path.
Profiled and tuned to hold per-execution latency under 50ns.
outcome: Backtesting tool for strategy evaluation at realistic execution-time latencies.

sideProjects.intro: Experiments and weekend builds — from recent LLM fine-tuning pipelines back through college-era systems work.

items.name: VoiceTune
items.description: Pipeline for turning personal audio call recordings into LoRA fine-tuning datasets for local LLMs — end-to-end from audio preprocessing to Gemma fine-tunes on GCP.

items.description: Django-backed image classification server running TensorFlow Inception v3, with a jQuery frontend for uploads and predictions.

items.description: Jupyter notebook demos of classic neural nets built from scratch — feedforward, CNN, RNN, autoencoders, Hopfield, and Restricted Boltzmann Machines.
